Module chariot
Package chariot

Interface Client

All Known Subinterfaces:
Client.Basic, ClientAuth
public sealed interface Client permits ClientAuth, Client.Basic
Provides access to the Lichess API.

There are two types of factory methods to create basic non-authenticated clients, Client, and authenticated clients, ClientAuth.

Examples of how to create a client

For accessing non-authenticated parts of the Lichess API: 

    Client client = Client.basic();

    // Tada!

    // And then use it...
    var user = client.users().byId("lichess");

For accessing authenticated parts of the Lichess API:
Note, a valid token must be acquired in order to use the endpoints exposed by ClientAuth, either by obtaining a Personal Access Token or by integrating a PKCE Authorization Code flow in the application - see Account for more information. 

    var token = ... // Token with scope email:read
    ClientAuth client = Client.auth(token);

    var email = client.account().emailAddress();

The responses from the APIs are modelled with a Result<T> "container".
Its documentation covers the types of responses (Result) and various ways of accessing their values (T).
The types of the values (T) used in the APIs are bundled in the chariot.model package - simple data holders / records.

An additional way of creating a client is via the load(prefs)/store(prefs) methods, keeping the configuration in Preferences, 

    Preferences prefs = Preferences.userRoot().node("myprefs");
    Client client = Client.load(prefs);
    if (client instanceof ClientAuth clientAuth) {
        // We've managed to restore our previously stored configuration,
        // so let's get right down to business.
        clientAuth.account().setKidModeStatus(true); // lol
    } else {
        // It seems this was the first time we ran this program,
        // we have yet to store its configuration.
        // Let's perform the heavy-lifting now.
        var urlToken = client.account().oauthPKCE(Scope.preference_write);
        System.out.println("Please grant this application access at Lichess: " + urlToken.url());

        // Wait for the user to go to Lichess and click Grant. (They wouldn't click Deny, right?)
        var token = urlToken.token().get();

        var clientAuth = Client.auth(token);

        // Now we can store the configuration for quick access next time.
        clientAuth.store(prefs);

        // Oh yeah, and...
        clientAuth.account().setKidModeStatus(true); // lol
    }

There are also customizable variants, 

    // http://localhost:9663
    var client = Client.basic(c -> c.local());

    var clientAuth = Client.auth(c -> c.api("https://lichess.dev").auth("mytoken"));

    Access Lichess cloud evaluations database.
    default Optional<ClientAuth>
    asAuth()
    Retrieves an Optional containing a ClientAuth if this is such a client, otherwise empty.
    static ClientAuth
    auth(String token)
    Creates a default client using the provided token to use the authenticated parts of the API
    static ClientAuth
    auth(Consumer<Builders.TokenBuilder> params)
    Creates a customizable client using the provided configuration parameters builder.
    Note, make sure to supply a token using the withToken* methods, or a IllegalArgumentException will be thrown.

    Creates a default client using the provided token to use the authenticated parts of the API

    Creates a customized client

  • Method Details

    • basic

      static Client basic()
      Creates a default client

    • auth

      static ClientAuth auth(String token)
      Creates a default client using the provided token to use the authenticated parts of the API
      Parameters:
      token - A token to use for the authenticated parts of the API

    • account

      Account account()
      Helps to perform a OAuth 2 PKCE flow or prepare a URL to create a Personal API Token with specified Scope/s.

    • analysis

      Analysis analysis()
      Access Lichess cloud evaluations database.

    • bot

      Bot bot()
      Access Lichess online bots.
      For more bot operations, see ClientAuth.bot()

    • broadcasts

      Broadcasts broadcasts()
      Relay chess events on Lichess.

      Official broadcasts are maintained by Lichess, but you can create your own broadcasts to cover any live game or chess event. You will need to publish PGN on a public URL so that Lichess can pull updates from it. Alternatively, you can push PGN updates to Lichess using this API.

      Broadcasts are organized in tournaments, which have several rounds, which have several games. You must first create a tournament, then you can add rounds to them.

    • challenges

      Challenges challenges()
      Open-ended challenges. For authenticated challenges, see ChallengesAuth

    • games

      Games games()
      Access games and TV channels, played on Lichess.

    • puzzles

      Puzzles puzzles()
      Access Lichess puzzle history and dashboard.

    • simuls

      Simuls simuls()
      Access simuls played on Lichess.

    • studies

      Studies studies()
      Access Lichess studies.

    • teams

      Teams teams()
      Access and manage Lichess teams and their members.

    • tournaments

      Tournaments tournaments()
      Access Arena and Swiss tournaments played on Lichess.

    • users

      Users users()
      Access registered users on Lichess.

      Stores the client configuration into the provided preferences node

      prefs - The preferences node to store this client configuration to

    • load

      static ClientAuth load(Preferences prefs, Consumer<Builders.AuthBuilder> token)
      Creates an authenticated customized client from a preferences node with provided token
      Parameters:
      prefs - A configuration preferences node
      token - A token to use for the authenticated parts of the API

    • reuseOrInitialize

      static ClientAuth reuseOrInitialize(Consumer<Builders.OAuthHelper> params)
      Helper method for initializing and storing an authorized client configuration.
      If the provided Preferences node already contains the token for the needed scopes, the authorized client will be returned immediately. If it doesn't contain such a token, the Lichess URL for a page to authorize the scopes will be written to standard output, for the user to grant access. If access is granted, the authorized client configuration will be stored in the Preferences node, and the authorized client is returned.
      The implementation of this helper method does this: 
        Preferences prefsNode = Preferences.userRoot().node(prefs);
  var client = Client.load(prefsNode);
  if (client instanceof ClientAuth auth &&
      auth.account().scopes().containsAll(Set.of(scopes)))
      return auth;

  var oauth = client.account().oauthPKCE(scopes);
  System.out.println("\n\nGrant access at URL:\n" + oauth.url() + "\n");
  var clientAuth = Client.load(prefs, auth -> auth.auth(oauth.token().get()));
  clientAuth.store(prefs);

  return clientAuth;
      Parameters:
      params - a functional interface with configuration, i.e Client.reuseOrInitialize(p -> p.prefs("my-prefs").scopes(Scope.team_read))
      Returns:
      a client authorized for the requested scope/s

    • reuseOrInitialize

      static ClientAuth reuseOrInitialize(Preferences prefs, Client.Scope... scopes)
      See reuseOrInitialize(Consumer)

    • asAuth

      default Optional<ClientAuth> asAuth()
      Retrieves an Optional containing a ClientAuth if this is such a client, otherwise empty.

    • logging

      default void logging(Consumer<Builders.LogSetter> params)
